title: WNBA: Urgent need to reach new CBA by Monday
content: WNBA commissioner Cathy Engelbert told reporters that there is urgency to get a new CBA done by Monday to avoid disruptions to the preseason calendar, including training camp and preseason games.

title: U.S. tops Canada, advances to showdown vs. D.R.
content: The United States booked its spot in the WBC semifinals with a 5-3 victory against Canada, joining the Dominican Republic team that mercy-ruled Korea in a 10-0 win in seven innings.

title: Bradley beats buzzer to put Arizona in Big 12 final
content: Jaden Bradley hit a fallaway jumper at the buzzer to send No. 2 Arizona to the Big 12 tournament final with an 82-80 win over Iowa State.

title: UCLA loses Bilodeau, takes out No. 8 Michigan St.
content: UCLA star Tyler Bilodeau suffered a right leg injury that will likely sideline him for at least one game, but the Bruins held off No. 8 Michigan State 88-84 to advance to the Big Ten tournament semifinals.

title: Leafs star Matthews (MCL) to miss rest of season
content: Maple Leafs captain Auston Matthews has a torn MCL in his left knee and will miss the rest of the NHL season.

title: Warriors lose 4 more to injury as slide continues
content: Warriors coach Steve Kerr said his team is "as beaten up as any team I can ever remember" after losing four more players to injury Friday night, when they lost their fourth straight to fall two games behind the Clippers for the West's eighth seed.

title: Wilson returns as Thorns secure NWSL opener win
content: Sophia Wilson's return and Olivia Moultrie's winning strike highlighted the National Women's Soccer League season opener in the Portland Thorns' victory over the Washington Spirit on Friday night.

title: MAC commish predicts 2 bids after Miami (OH) loss
content: MAC commissioner Jon A. Steinbrecher is confident his conference will get two teams into the NCAA tournament for the first time in 27 years despite Miami (Ohio) losing to UMass in the league quarterfinals after going 31-0 in the regular season.

title: Men's Bracketology: The SEC teams that will sweat out Selection Sunday
content: Texas, Oklahoma and Auburn are all hanging around the cutline. Only the Sooners can currently control their destiny.

content: The Bruins held on to beat the Spartans in the Big Ten quarterfinals to solidify their NCAA tournament résumé.

title: Women's Bracketology: Bubble fate could boil down to Princeton and Ivy final
content: Richmond, Nebraska and Arizona State will breathe easier if Princeton wins the Ivy and keeps it a one-bid league.

content: The order for all seven rounds of the 2026 NFL draft is set, starting with the Raiders at No. 1 and ending with the Broncos at No. 257.

title: 'Just wild': Bam Adebayo's 83-point performance resonated with his AAU coach
content: Ty White said his former high school star did something to wow him every game, but even this performance has him stunned.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Man United have five-man shortlist to replace Carrick
content: Manchester United are reported to be looking at five managers to replace interim boss Michael Carrick in the summer. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Hamilton: Ferrari losing 'a lot of time' to Mercedes
content: Lewis Hamilton has said Ferrari's gap to Mercedes is mainly due to a deficit in power unit performance after he qualified fourth for Saturday's Chinese Grand Prix sprint race.

content: Tristan da Silva is on the rise, and should fantasy basketball managers keep players like Bennedict Mathurin and Cameron Johnson?

content: You've read their names all preseason long, it'd be a shame to forget them on draft day. The ESPN Cheat Sheet is one way to make sure that doesn't happen.

content: Your one-stop shop for fantasy football reaction to all the key player movement via free agency and trades.

title: Chincholi to be developed as mini tourist circuit, says Priyank Kharge
content: He says several tourist attractions in and around Chincholi, including Chandampalli reservoir, Gottangotta area and Ettipoth waterfalls, will be integrated into a single tourism circuit.

title: Mayawati accuses Congress of misusing Kanshi Ram's name for political gains
content: The Congress never gave due honour to Babasaheb Dr Bhimrao Ambedkar -- the messiah of Dalits and the principal architect of the Constitution -- nor did they ever confer upon him the title of Bharat Ratna... How can the same party now honour Kanshi Ram, questiones BSP chief Mayawati

title: Govt. takes steps to construct seven greenfield airports, including one in Amaravati
content: The total estimated cost is pegged at ₹7,472 crore and 4,500 acres of land for the facility in the capital city is proposed to be allotted under LPS-2

title: With condolence visit to Iran Embassy, Akali Dal’s ‘Panthic’ politics in play ahead of 2027 Assembly polls
content: Earlier this week, the SAD delegation led by its Delhi unit president Paramjit Singh Sarna visited the Iranian Embassy and extended heartfelt sympathies following the killing of Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei

title: T.N. announces ₹2-per-unit electricity subsidy for hotels, cloud kitchens using electric stoves
content: The decision was taken at a meeting chaired by Chief Minister M.K. Stalin to discuss the crisis caused by the lack of adequate LPG in the wake of the war in West Asia

title: Mass copying at school in Himachal's Kangra; teacher caught dictating answers, suspended
content: A preliminary inquiry revealed that a teacher inside the examination hall was verbally dictating answers to multiple-choice questions to students

title: Two Indian vessels carrying LPG cross Strait of Hormuz safely: Shipping Ministry official
content: LPG carriers Shivalik and Nanda Devi are now headed to Mundra and Kandla ports in Gujarat, Rajesh Kumar Sinha, Special Secretary in Ministry of Shipping, told a media briefing

title: 2 Maoists arrested in Jharkhand’s Latehar
content: The two, jointly carrying a bounty of ₹12 lakh, were wanted in connection with several cases registered at police stations in districts and states, including Chhattisgarh, the Latehar SP said

title: Bengal's farmer-friendly initiative benefited 1.4 crore people: CM Mamata Banerjee
content: Under the scheme, beneficiaries receive ₹10,000 annually, while those with very small landholdings receive a minimum of ₹4,000. The amount is transferred directly to bank accounts in two instalments during the Kharif and Rabi seasons, says West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee

title: Dry fruit shop supervisor kills live-in partner, tries to pass it off as suicide
content: The victim, Ranjitha (24), a native of Ponnampet in Kodagu district, was found dead at a rented house in Kodathi area

title: Ernakulam has domestic LPG stock for 25 days, consumers need not panic, says Collector
content: After an emergency meeting with stakeholders, the Collector said that while increased bookings have caused technical issues for oil companies, which are being resolved, there is no need for panic.

title: Centre releases another ₹640 crore for Telangana Gram Panchayats
content: Panchayat Raj Minister urges Centre to release remaining arrears of Financial Year 2025-26 as 15th Finance Commission term set to end in 15 days

title: Karur stampede: TVK chief Vijay leaves for Delhi to appear for CBI inquiry
content: On Saturday, Mr. Vijay left for the capital along with party functionaries Aadhav Arjuna, C.T.R. Nirmal Kumar, and others from the Chennai airport

title: Udupi Excise Deputy Commissioner accused of possessing disproportionate assets
content: In a press note, the Lokayukta police said search was carried out at the official’s house, office and a total of his 15 places on March 11

title: ₹10 crore sanctioned for land acquisition for Kadalundi railway overbridge
content: Officials said the funds had been allotted to the PWD Bridges Wing. Approximately 0.9037 hectares of land will be acquired for the project

title: Watch: India must find sources of sustainable energy to curb inflation | The Hindu Editorial
content: Inflation is being driven by supply constraints, so trying to reduce demand by raising interest rates will not only have a minimal impact on inflation but could also further hurt growth when fuel constraints are already impacting it. The onus lies with the government and its efforts to expedite alternative sources of fuel.

title: Real estate businessman in Karnataka arrested for cheating temple devotees of ₹2.15 lakh with government loan scheme claim
content: The police officials said that the accused has been identified as Somashekhar H.K. (46), a real estate businessman from Hassan

title: Tamil Nadu govt. appoints senior IPS officer Bala Naga Devi as DGP, Intelligence
content: Last December, she was promoted as DGP, Economic Offences Wing, and was also holding the post of DGP, Civil Supplies CID

title: Nearly 2.37 lakh domestic cylinders delivered in Telangana on March 13; 12,000 more than daily average
content: The daily requirement of the LPG cylinders in Telangana is 2.5 lakh, of which 86% is for domestic and the remaining 14% is commercial purposes

title: A cradle of care: How Kerala fights infant mortality
content: The story of Kerala being the State with the lowest Infant Mortality Rate is that of a combined effort, timely interventions and the potential inherited from a glorious history

title: Govt. working to minimise impact of West Asia war on citizens, Congress fuelling panic: PM
content: During a gathering in poll-bound Assam, Prime Minister Narendra Modi says it was expected that the Congress would play the role of a responsible political party, but it failed yet again to prioritise national interest

title: Tadikonda MRO, three other Revenue officers suspended
content: Guntur district Collector Tameem Ansaria issued orders taking action against the MRO and other Revenue officers

title: Around 20 injured, as van overturns in Alluri Sitharama Raju district
content: According to the police, around 20 workers from Jami in Vizianagaram district had been engaged in railway-related works in Ananthagiri mandal

title: Kerala Assembly elections: High command will decide on MPs contesting, says Satheesan
content: Opposition leader dismisses speculation over Sudhakaran’s Facebook post; declares more surprises are in store

title: Musi Riverfront in Hyderabad to house East-West Corridor in BOT model, and Gandhi Sarovar in 200 acres
content: The riverfront will be divided into five zones for development, with zone-1 up to Bapu Ghat being taken up in phase-I. Gandhi Sarovar-Puranapul will be Zone-2, Puranapul-MGBS, Zone-3, MGBS-Uppal/Nagole, Zone-4, and from there up to Gowrelly will be Zone-5

title: AAP government drowned Punjab in corruption, drugs and crime: Amit Shah
content: Punjab's Moga rally is being seen as the beginning of the BJP's poll campaign in Punjab, where assembly elections are due in early 2027

title: Lok Adalat resolves disputes in Andhra Pradesh’s Guntur
content: An official said the initiative aims to ensure swift, accessible and affordable justice for common people through amicable settlements

title: Video: US Hammers Iran's Kharg Island, Over 90 Targets Destroyed In Strikes
content: The United States forces executed a large-scale precision strike on Iran's Kharg Island, its biggest oil export hub, on Friday night, the US Central Command said on Saturday.

title: "Orphan Pearl" On Fire: The Critical Oil Island Struck By US In Iran
content: US Strikes Iran: Spread over a meagre 20 square kilometres, Iran's Kharg island processes 90 per cent of the country's total oil exports, driving the region to the edge as the US attacked it on Friday, 15 days into the Middle East conflict.

title: Bodycam Shows Israeli Troops Raid Beirut, South Lebanon In Night Missions
content: The Israel Defence Forces (IDF) on Thursday released body-cam footage showing an operation targeting sites that they alleged were linked to the militant group Hezbollah in Beirut and southern Lebanon.

title: Major UAE Oil Terminal In Flames Hours After US Strikes Iran's Kharg Island
content: Dark smoke was seen rising on Saturday from a major energy installation in the United Arab Emirates. This comes hours after the United States carried out strikes on Iran's Kharg Island oil hub.

title: 'India Has Adequate Fertiliser Stocks': Government Sources Amid Iran's Hormuz Chokehold
content: The Iran conflict has disrupted supply routes through the Strait of Hormuz, a key corridor for global fertiliser shipments, pushing up prices of crop nutrients such as urea and ammonia and raising import costs for major buyers like India.

title: 'It Was Suicide': Bengaluru Man Told Lover's Parents. Then, A Murder Twist
content: A 23-year-old woman was allegedly murdered by her live-in partner and it was made to look like a suicide in Bengaluru's Varthur police limits.

title: Tamil Lyricist Vairamuthu Named 2025 Jnanpith Award Winner
content: The 60th Jnanpith Award, India's highest literary honour, for the year 2025 will be conferred on distinguished Tamil poet, lyricist and writer R. Vairamuthu.

title: Opinion: Opinion: When It Counted, India Stood With The UAE
content: The current crisis has not weakened the UAE, and it will not. Instead, it has demonstrated the depth of the UAE's national structure, the resilience of its institutions, the cohesion of its society, and the strength of its international standing.

title: 2 India-Bound Ships Cross Hormuz After 4 Rounds Of Phone Calls, PM's Outreach
content: Two India-flagged Shipping Corporation of India (SCI) LPG tankers have crossed the Strait of Hormuz today and are headed towards India.

title: 'Hearts Sink When We See Drones, Jet': Indian Sailors Stranded Off Iran
content: Stranded for two weeks at the Iranian port of Bandar Abbas as drones and missiles set ships ablaze nearby, 26-year-old seafarer Ambuj says he has not been home for six months and cannot wait to see his family.

title: Drug-Free State, Bill Against Conversion: Amit Shah's Poll Pitch In Punjab
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah on Saturday launched a sharp attack on the Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) government in Punjab while addressing a rally in Moga, declaring that the BJP has begun its campaign for the 2027 Punjab Assembly elections.

title: The Other Route Options For Global Shipping If Hormuz Remains Blocked
content: As the Israel-US war on Iran disrupts shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, alternative sea routes may become important for global trade and energy supplies.

title: "Where's Bibi?" '6 Fingers' Video Sparks Concerns About Netanyahu
content: Social media posts raised questions about the whereabouts of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Saturday as several users flagged concerns over his latest March 13 video which made it seem like he had six fingers in one of his hands.

title: US-Israel-Iran War LIVE: Deaths Of IRIS Dena Crew Members "Won't Go Unanswered", Warns Iran 's Army Chief
content: US-Israel-Iran War News LIVE Updates: Iran's Army chief has issued a stern warning that the deaths of crew members from the IRIS Dena warship in the Indian Ocean "will not go unanswered", as reported by Al Jazeera, citing Iran's state news agency.

title: Dinosaur Fossils In Brazil Reveal New Giant Species
content: Brazilian scientists have identified a new species of giant dinosaur with ties to a similar animal found in Spain, reinforcing knowledge that land routes once connected parts of South America, Africa and Europe about 120 million years ago.

title: 56 Museums, Historic Sites Damaged, Says Iran As War Enters Day 15
content: Iran's cultural heritage and tourism ministry said Saturday at least 56 museums and historic sites across the country have been damaged, as the Middle East war entered its 15th day.

title: Drunk Man Thrashed For Urinating Near Women In Chennai. Friend Dies Saving Him
content: The victim, Kalaiselvan, and his friend, Nithish Balaji, were travelling to Mannadi in a vehicle. The two had consumed alcohol, officials said.

title: "President Wants You": Trump Summons US Treasury Secretary Mid Live Interview
content: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent left a live Sky News interview after being urgently summoned to meet US President Donald Trump at the White House.

title: Punjab Man Leaves For Russia For Job, Arrives In Coffin 8 Months Later
content: Samarjit Singh travelled to Russia in July 2025 through recruitment agents. They claim he was lured with promises of a high-paying job, but upon arrival, he was allegedly forcibly conscripted into the Russian army, according to his family.

title: "Injustice": Sole Muslim Woman On Trump's Religious Panel Quits Over Iran War
content: Sameerah Munshi, appointed by Trump in 2025, announced she was stepping down because of the "injustice and atrocities" of the Trump administration both in the United States and abroad.

title: Lone elephant driven back into forest after 48-hour operation in Erode
content: During the operation, the elephant crossed three major roads, including the busy Sathyamangalam-Gobichettipalayam road

title: LPG shortage sparks surge in firewood demand in Kerala
content: Hotels and eateries turn to alternative fuel as commercial cylinder supply dries up; sawmills and plywood units report rising inquiries amid price hike

title: Karnataka High Court upholds KPTCL regulations entrusting departmental inquiries to Lokayukta
content: The single judge had held that conduct of both the investigation and the departmental inquiry by the officers of the Lokayukta amounts to “inevitable bias” against the employee and offends Article 14 of the Constitution of India

title: Centre revokes detention of climate activist Sonam Wangchuk under NSA
content: Government has decided to revoke the detention of Sonam Wangchuk with immediate effect by exercising the powers available under the National Security Act; he was released from Jodhpur jail later

title: Case against school after children being allegedly forced to stand in blazing sun in Yelahanka
content: The incident reportedly occurred at a school in Attur Layout, where parents alleged that young children were forced to remain standing outside in the blazing sun as punishment

title: Nine-judge SC Bench to hear arguments on March 17 on definition of 'industry'
content: A seven-judge Constitution Bench headed by then CJI T.S. Thakur in 2017 said it was of the opinion that the appeals before it be placed before a Bench comprising nine judges keeping in view the "serious and wide-ranging implications" of the issue

title: Telangana Civil Supplies Minister to meet with LPG Distributors Association on March 14
content: People were urged to dial toll free number 1967 to report hoarding or lodge grievances pertaining to the supply

title: Lt Gen (Retd) Syed Ata Hasnain sworn in as Bihar Governor
content: Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar, both deputy Chief Ministers and cabinet ministers attended the oath taking ceremony; Mr. Hasnain took the oath as the 43rd Governor of Bihar since India’s independence

title: Several Maharashtra Cities Record Temperatures Above 40°C; Rain and Thunderstorms Predicted from March 17
content: Regions including Vidarbha, Marathwada, Khandesh, and parts of Central Maharashtra are expected to be affected by rain, thunderstorms, and gusty winds between March 17 and 20

title: Petrol, diesel, and domestic LPG supply in the State remains normal, says Haryana Civil Supplies Minister
content: “Hundreds of thousands of cylinders were being distributed daily across the State through a network of over 5,000 distributors,” says Minister

title: MRTS train services begin on Velachery-St. Thomas Mount extended section
content: People who reside in Nanganallur, Puzhuthivakkam, Adambakkam, Madipakkam, and adjoining areas will benefit from this service

title: Debut in politics, Nishant Kumar remains busy with party activities
content: Junior Mr. Kumar who is likely to become one of the new Deputy Chief Ministers of the State is visiting Temples, Gurudwara, Mazar and various other locations to pay homage to eminent leaders to seek their blessings after joining the party

title: Bhopal gas tragedy: Madhya Pradesh HC asks government to share clean-up plan
content: In an action taken report, the government told the court that a meeting chaired by Additional Chief Secretary Ashok Barnwal was held on March 3 to discuss a time-bound remediation of the Union Carbide India Ltd. (UCIL) factory

title: Rahul Gandhi pays tribute to Kanshi Ram ahead of birth anniversary, vows to fight for marginalised
content: “Today we remember Kanshi Ram ji’s struggle, his vision, and his influence on Indian politics. I thank him wholeheartedly. We pledge to follow his path and fight for the issues of marginalised sections. If the Congress had functioned effectively at that time, Kanshi Ram would not have needed to wage a separate struggle,” Rahul Gandhi

title: Attack on Manipur woman: NHRC issues notice to Delhi Police
content: Police apprehended four minors for the attack, in which she suffered injuries to her face, and for hurling racial and sexual slurs at her

title: 11 Indian nationals charged in U.S. over visa fraud scheme
content: The charge of conspiracy to commit visa fraud provides for a sentence of up to five years in prison, three years of supervised release and a fine of $250,000

title: Pi Day 2026: significance of the mathematical constant π
content: March 14 is celebrated globally as Pi Day as the first digits of the mathematical constant π, 3.14159, match the date 3/14

title: The curious case of SCR’s twin stations
content: From Lallaguda to Ammuguda, South Central Railway hosts rare “twin stations” sharing identical names yet serving different roles and routes. While some function purely as operational signalling points, others handle passengers, creating unique railway geography and occasional commuter confusion

title: What are the changes being proposed to Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Act, 2019 | Explained
content: How is the government proposing to alter the definition of “transgender person”? Why has it argued for the omission of the right to “self-perceived gender identity”? What about the proposed changes have raised concerns among the trans community? What has the Supreme Court laid down in the landmark 2014 judgement recognising a third gender beyond male and female and the right to self-determination of gender identity?

title: India roots for benefits system at pandemic agreement talks
content: India has found common cause with a coalition of developing countries that says developing countries that share pathogen materials and genetic sequence data must receive fair, concrete, and legally enforceable benefits in return

title: CM Mamata announces development boards for marginalised communities ahead of Bengal Assembly polls
content: The announcement comes days after President Droupadi Murmu stated that the West Bengal government was not serious about development of Scheduled Tribes in the State

title: Restored Kamarajar Salai in Arcot town opened for public use
content: Municipal officials said that a key feature of the restoration of the stretch was an increase in its total height by two feet to the existing level of stormwater drains; this is to avoid inundation

title: New District Munsif and Judicial Magistrate Court near Katpadi inaugurated
content: Officials of the Public Works Department, which completed the work, said that the new court will function inside the refurbished panchayat office premises in the village

title: Udhayanidhi Stalin to inaugurate lounge for construction workers, village knowledge centre in Tirupattur
content: Spread over 350 sq.ft, the all-weather lounge is fitted with fans, benches, mobile phone charging units, and drinking water facilities

title: Congregational prayers, rallies held in Kashmir on Ramzan’s last Friday; Jamia Masjid closed
content: The Mirwaiz said it’s for the seventh consecutive year that the rulers have denied Muslims the right to offer prayers at Jama Masjid on this auspicious day

title: Seven dead, 92 inmates infected with herpes virus at overcrowded Jalpaiguri correctional home
content: About 92 inmates have been infected in the outbreak of the Herpes Simplex Virus between August 20, 2025 and March 9, 2026. Seven persons infected by the virus have passed away.  The number of inmates at correctional home with a capacity 700 is over 1200

title: West Asia crisis: The Indians caught in the crossfire
content: Since February 28, when Israel and the U.S. jointly attacked Iran, plunging the entire West Asian region into war, thousands of Indian tourists and expatriates have been left stranded across the Gulf. Indian missions have struggled to keep up with requests and demands. From missile and drone strikes, to soaring prices, to communication blackouts, the conflict has left civilians vulnerable, reports Kallol Bhattacherjee

title: Retention rate below 50% in BMC schools, finds report
content: According to the report, titled ‘State of Municipal Education in Mumbai’, during the period, the transition of students from Class 8 to Class 9 declined by 30%, and only 80% of students moved from Class 9 to Class 10

title: Exercise LAMITIYE-2026: India and Seychelles carry out joint military exercise
content: A series of professional interactions, tactical discussions and practical drills have been conducted as part of the exercise. Operational demonstrations have been a key component of LAMITIYE-2026.

title: IndiGo to levy fuel charge for domestic and international flights from March 14
content: IndiGo said it would continue to monitor the situation and make relevant adjustments as and when appropriate.

title: Opposition leaders press government, Lok Sabha Speaker to revoke the suspension of 8 MPs
content: The eight MPs, including seven from the Congress, have been barred from attending the remainder of the Budget Session

title: Maharashtra govt. tables Bill to ban ‘unlawful conversions’
content: It proposes stringent provisions for conversions carried out through coercion, fraud, inducement, or marriage; objective to protect right to freedom of religion, says Minister while introducing Bill

title: Futuristic mindfulness city fuels Bhutan’s ‘fly east’ via Assam
content: Drukair says the commencement of Paro-Singapore flight via Guwahati makes commercial sense because of altitude-dictated weight restrictions that force aeroplanes to take off from or land at Paro Airport with less fuel

title: Chennai Corporation launches projects, expedites works ahead of poll
content: Greater Chennai Corporation (GCC) has expedited civic infrastructure projects, including work on 17 bridges, in various zones of the city

title: Stalin announces pension, family pension hike for journalists
content: M.K.Stalin also directed officials to take steps to provide medical insurance under the Chief Minister’s Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme to all journalists holding accredited identity cards

title: Kerala Cabinet approves Film Policy
content: It was prepared after discussions with stakeholders in the film industry, ideas shared at the Film Conclave and opinions gathered from the public

title: VCK hopes to keep its ‘recognised party’ tag
content: Party leaders also feel a higher allotment of seats would justify its consistent support to the DMK front since 2017

title: Nitish Kumar hints at Samrat Choudhary as his successor in Bihar
content: During the ongoing Samriddhi Yatra in Saharsa, the Chief Minister placed his hands on Mr. Choudhary’s shoulders and urged the public to show their support to the Deputy CM

title: Fuel crisis: hoteliers advised to trim menus, cut operating hours
content: Finance Minister suggests alternate day operations as hotel bodies seek at least 50% quota of commercial LPG cylinders to sustain operations in the State

title: Kerala Cabinet approves formulation of land assignment rules for use of pattaya land for other purposes
content: Fees will be waived for houses and for buildings constructed for agricultural and educational purposes, as well as markets, places of worship, structures for public purposes, cultural institutions, clubs and structures for charitable establishments

title: Haryana Rajya Sabha polls: Congress MLAs reach Himachal Pradesh to prevent cross-voting
content: After CLP meet, about 30 MLAs leave for Shimla; party insiders say the move is meant to keep the flock together ahead of three-cornered election; Congress holds 37 seats and needs 31 votes to send its candidate

title: US shares video of strikes on 90 Iranian military targets on Kharg Island - watch
content: United States President Donald Trump said American forces carried out “the most powerful bombing raid” on Iran’s Kharg Island, claiming that American forces “totally obliterated” military targets there while choosing not to destroy the island’s oil infrastructure. ​​Any disruption at Kharg Island can significantly affect global oil supply routes, making it a critical node for global energy security and oil market stability.

title: 'Openly protecting rapists': PM Modi slams TMC over RG Kar and Sandeshkhali cases
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi launched a strong attack on the TMC in Kolkata, accusing the party of protecting rapists and insulting President Droupadi Murmu. He also alleged that the TMC opposes electoral roll revisions to shield infiltrators and deliberately alters demographics, making Hindus a minority in several areas.

title: 'Bumrah is Usman Tariq of fast bowlers': Pak selector makes shocking claim
content: Pakistan selector Aaqib Javed sparked debate after comparing Indian pacer Jasprit Bumrah to Usman Tariq, citing his unusual bowling action. Bumrah starred in the ICC Men's T20 World Cup, finishing joint-highest wicket-taker with 14 wickets alongside Varun Chakravarthy. His 4/15 in the final against New Zealand national cricket team helped India secure a dominant 96-run victory.

title: Cognizant advises BYOF for employees, reviews supply concerns
content: Cognizant is encouraging employees to bring their own food as part of contingency planning amid the West Asia crisis. The IT major is preparing for potential disruptions in food services and transport due to energy market volatility. The company is exploring alternative vendors and energy sources to mitigate risks.

title: PCB to probe into Babar Azam, Fakhar Zaman after T20 World Cup exit
content: Pakistan's selectors are demanding an inquiry into how star players Babar Azam and Fakhar Zaman sustained injuries, with concerns raised that fitness issues were not disclosed during the T20 World Cup.  Aaqib Javed questioned if players would have been declared fit had Pakistan advanced, highlighting a lack of transparency.  Younger players are now being given opportunities.

title: Inside Iran's war tunnel: Huge collection of drones displayed under Khamenei's images - watch
content: Iran's Revolutionary Guard launched its 48th wave of retaliatory strikes, "Operation True Promise 4," targeting Israeli and US interests. State media showcased drone launches and an underground arsenal, reporting attacks on northern Israel, including Haifa, and American bases. The IRGC claimed to have hit Qatar's al-Udaid airbase, asserting it caused panic in Israel.

title: Accenture CEO Julie Sweet to company’s employees: If you want a promotion, you need to ...
content: Accenture CEO Julie Sweet has mandated that employees must demonstrate proficiency in using AI tools to be considered for promotions. This requirement reflects the consultancy's integration of AI into its core operations, a shift Sweet likens to the adoption of computers in the past. The company has invested significantly in reskilling its workforce to adapt to these evolving workplace technologies.

title: UP woman ‘revived’ by jolt on NH-74: NHAI says ‘no potholes’ on stretch but ground reality different
content: A woman critically ill and believed to be brain dead revived after the ambulance carrying her home hit a pothole-ridden stretch of National Highway-74. Despite NHAI claims of a pothole-free road, a TOI team found damaged patches, including cracks and depressions, on the route.

title: No Pakistan player fined for T20 World Cup exit: PCB
content: The Pakistan Cricket Board has refuted claims of fining players for not reaching the T20 World Cup semifinals. A spokesperson clarified that while no fines have been imposed, the board is considering performance-linked incentives for players, who currently earn substantial amounts.

title: India’s Nobel paradox: Why Mahatma Gandhi remains the most famous non-winner
content: Despite India's rich intellectual tradition and early Nobel laureates like Tagore and Raman, the country has experienced long gaps in recognition. Factors like brain drain, limited funding, and an emphasis on publications over long-term innovation hinder the journey from research to global acclaim. The case of Mahatma Gandhi's missed Peace Prize also highlights complexities beyond merit.

title: Agha run-out controversy: B'desh captain justifies the act in 2nd ODI
content: Pakistan leveled their ODI series against Bangladesh with a dominant win, but a controversial run-out of Salman Ali Agha stole the spotlight. Bangladesh captain Mehidy Hasan Miraz defended his actions, stating Agha was out of his crease and a potential run was on. Despite the debate, Pakistan's bowlers secured a convincing victory.

title: 'How I finally got out of Dubai': Traveller shares tips after flight cancellations
content: A traveler, stranded in Dubai due to Gulf Air flight cancellations linked to Bahrain airspace issues, shared her ordeal. After a week of disruptions, she finally departed on an Emirates flight. She advises monitoring flight patterns, visiting airline offices in person for better options, and remaining adaptable amidst ongoing travel challenges in the region.

title: Sathyanathan breaks national record, wins gold at NCAA Indoor C'ships
content: Indian long jumper Lokesh Sathyanathan won gold at the NCAA Division I Indoor Track and Field Championships in Fayetteville, Arkansas with an 8.21m leap, setting a new Indian indoor record. The Tarleton State University athlete finished ahead of De'Aundre Ward and Tafadzwa Chikomba. Lokesh dedicated the milestone to his late mother, who died from COVID-19 in 2021.

title: Salman Ali Agha reprimanded after controversial run-out in 2nd ODI
content: Pakistan batter Salman Ali Agha was reprimanded after the second ODI against Bangladesh national cricket team at Sher-e-Bangla National Cricket Stadium. He was controversially run out by Mehidy Hasan Miraz after trying to pick up a live ball. Frustrated, Agha threw his gloves and argued with Litton Das, leading to a Level 1 breach under the International Cricket Council Code. Pakistan still won by 128 runs via DLS.

title: 'I apologise': Trump aide Laura Loomer apologises for 'anti-India' posts on social media
content: Far-right activist Laura Loomer apologised for "anti-India" social media posts after facing backlash for racist remarks. While she expressed regret for offending people, Loomer stood firm on her opposition to the H-1B visa program, citing a need to protect American workers. She also criticized Pakistan, accusing it of supporting extremist networks.

title: The Hundred: Will PAK allow Abrar to earn PKR 7 crore during bilateral commitments?
content: Abrar Ahmed, the Pakistan spinner, was bought by Sunrisers Leeds, a franchise controlled by the owners of IPL's Sunrisers Hyderabad, for £190,000 (around PKR 7 crore) on Thursday during The Hundred. However, the mystery spinner may not receive a No Objection Certificate (NOC), as the Pakistan national cricket team is scheduled to tour West Indies for a two-match Test series during that period.

title: Irked over charity spend, couple kills mother in Bengal, dumps body in sack
content: A shocking incident unfolded in Howrah where a son and his wife were arrested for allegedly murdering his 62-year-old mother. The victim's body was discovered in a sack. The son confessed to the crime, citing his mother's extensive charitable activities as a financial strain. He claimed his wife was also mistreated by the victim, leading to the brutal act.

title: ‘Upset with girlfriend’, Maharashtra man hurls stones at passing train
content: A 23-year-old man in Kalyan allegedly threw stones at a Vande Bharat Express, damaging four coaches. The incident occurred between Thakurli and Kalyan stations after the man reportedly became upset when his girlfriend refused to speak to him on the phone. He admitted to the act, claiming drug consumption. No passengers were injured, but panic ensued.

title: Village throws party for ‘IAS officer’, later discover UPSC rank belongs to someone else
content: What began as a joyous occasion in a Bihar village quickly spiraled into a tale of disappointment when Ranjeet Kumar's announcement of conquering the UPSC civil services exam was revealed to be an elaborate ruse.

title: MS Dhoni's last IPL season? Ex-cricketer makes big prediction
content: Former India all-rounder Irfan Pathan suggests the upcoming IPL season might be MS Dhoni's final appearance for Chennai Super Kings. While Dhoni's fitness is praised, his role is expected to shift towards mentoring, with Sanju Samson potentially benefiting from his guidance. The team aims for a sixth IPL trophy as a fitting farewell.

title: Attackers dance after hacking Bengaluru man 68 times with iron straps
content: A fruit vendor, Santosh R, miraculously survived a brutal assault of 68 blows with iron straps on a Bengaluru road. He is an accused in the murder of the attacker Suresh's brother. Suresh and three associates, fueled by revenge, ambushed Santosh after he appeared in court for the murder trial. The attackers fled after the violent incident.

title: TMC and BJP supporters clash in Kolkata hours before PM Modi’s rally
content: Tensions flared near Girish Park in Kolkata as TMC and BJP supporters clashed, pelting stones and raising slogans just before Prime Minister Modi's rally. Both parties accused each other of initiating the violence, with claims of damaged vehicles and hurled abuses. Police intervened to restore order in the area.

title: After IndiGo and Air India, now Akasa Air to levy fuel surcharge - check details
content: Indian airlines are increasing ticket prices due to soaring aviation fuel costs, driven by Middle East geopolitical tensions. Akasa Air, following Air India and IndiGo, will implement fuel surcharges from March 15th, ranging from Rs 199 to Rs 1,300.

title: PCB mulls legal action after Muzarabani opts for IPL over PSL: Report
content: Pakistan Cricket Board is reportedly considering legal action against Zimbabwe's Blessing Muzarabani. He opted to play for Kolkata Knight Riders in the IPL instead of fulfilling his contract with Islamabad United in the Pakistan Super League. This move, driven by IPL's lucrative opportunities, has angered the PCB, who aim to protect the integrity of their league contracts.

title: Akshaye Khanna was 'not nice on set,' stayed irritable over hair loss, reveals Farah Khan
content: Farah Khan revealed on Ranveer Allahbadia’s podcast that working with Akshaye Khanna in the ’90s left her traumatised due to his irritability over hair loss. She said he changed after Dil Chahta Hai. Farah recently praised him for Dhurandhar and recalled how others rejected his Tees Maar Khan role before he took it.

title: ‘Surviving loan by loan’: Pak's wallet could take another hit from soaring oil prices
content: Pakistan grapples with an escalating economic crisis as Middle East conflict disrupts vital oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.  Soaring fuel prices are impacting farmers, urban commuters, and households, forcing difficult choices.  With the nation already financially strained, prolonged energy shortages threaten to destabilize its economy further, affecting daily life and traditional celebrations.

title: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk to be released as govt revokes NSA detention
content: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk's detention under the National Security Act has been revoked by the Centre with immediate effect. Wangchuk was detained over allegations of inciting unrest in Ladakh, with the government citing his remarks as potentially destabilizing. His wife had challenged the detention in the Supreme Court.

title: Women, kids among 40 trapped inside dilapidated building as new owner razes stairs in Kolkata
content: Around 40 residents were trapped in a dilapidated Kolkata building after the staircase was allegedly razed by the new owner. Emergency services used ladders to rescue many, including women and children, from the building slated for real-estate development. The mayor assured tenant protection, while residents expressed concerns about displacement.

title: 'We're going to create ... ': Suryakumar Yadav pens emotional note
content: Suryakumar Yadav celebrates five years in international cricket, a journey marked by a remarkable T20 World Cup triumph.  His impactful debut in 2022 paved the way for a career defined by explosive batting and astute leadership.  Yadav's impressive stats and captaincy record highlight his significant contributions to Team India's success.

title: Strait‑up chokehold of Hormuz: How Iran turned Middle East war into global oil supply shock
content: Iran has strategically prepared to leverage the Strait of Hormuz, employing asymmetric warfare with drones and missiles to disrupt global oil flows. This approach, learned from past conflicts, aims to inflict economic pain and pressure the US, rather than engage in direct confrontation.  The strategy focuses on survival and demonstrating that coercion cannot force compliance.

title: Robberies grow bolder and more organised with guns, knives and deception
content: Despite the digital age, India witnesses daring real-life heists targeting large cash movements and businesses. From Delhi's Chandni Chowk to Surat's highways and Karnataka's banks, armed gangs employ deception, intimidation, and violence in carefully planned robberies, highlighting a persistent challenge for law enforcement.

title: Gangster groom arrested from marriage venue in Bhopal; fiancée pleads for his release
content: A notorious criminal, Akash alias Bhura Haddi, was arrested on his wedding night for orchestrating a honey trap and extortion scheme. He and his gang abducted a priest, assaulted him, and forced his brother to transfer Rs 50,000. They threatened to frame the priest in a rape case if he didn't pay an additional Rs 8 lakh.

title: Class VI students check Class X papers? Maharashtra Board to re-evaluate
content: The Kolhapur division of the Maharashtra Board has requested an inquiry into a viral video allegedly showing Std VI students evaluating Std X Marathi exam papers. A preliminary report suggests the children were marking blank sections to prevent cheating, not grading. Officials will visit the school to verify claims and teacher authorization.

title: Husband away since youngest’s birth, mom kills four daughters in Madhya Pradesh
content: A woman in Sagar district allegedly drowned her four daughters in a farm well before taking her own life. Police are investigating domestic tension, possibly linked to the birth of multiple daughters, as a motive. The husband had reportedly been absent since the youngest child's birth.

title: Did Trump reject Putin's Iran deal to end war? What was the offer
content: Russia's Vladimir Putin reportedly proposed Russia take custody of Iran's enriched uranium to end the ongoing war, a deal Donald Trump declined. This offer comes as the US and Israel seek to secure Iran's nuclear stockpile, with options ranging from voluntary surrender to potential special forces intervention. Trump, however, downplayed the immediate focus on the uranium.

title: America’s low-cost reply to Iran’s Shahed drones? US launches LUCAS from aircraft carrier - watch
content: The US Navy has successfully launched its new Low-cost Uncrewed Combat Attack System (LUCAS) drone from a ship, marking a significant shift in warfare. Developed by reverse-engineering Iranian drones, these affordable 'kamikaze' systems are designed for mass deployment to overwhelm enemy defenses, as seen in Operation Epic Fury against Iran.

title: Explosion damages Jewish school in Amsterdam; mayor calls it targeted attack
content: An explosion rocked a Jewish school in Amsterdam, with the mayor suspecting a targeted attack on the community. This follows a similar incident at a Rotterdam synagogue, where arson is suspected. Authorities detained teenagers near another synagogue. An Islamist group has claimed responsibility for both Dutch incidents and a prior attack in Belgium, raising concerns.

title: 'He is riding his luck': Uthappa's big comment on Sooryavanshi ahead of IPL 2026
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, after a stellar IPL debut, faces heightened expectations and increased scrutiny in his second season. Former India cricketer Robin Uthappa suggests this year will be a crucial learning phase as opponents have analyzed his game. While expecting him to score, Uthappa believes the element of surprise has faded, making this season pivotal for his long-term development.

content: alysa Liu won the first winter games gold medal by an american woman in more than two decades . she won gold in the women's singles competition and again as part of the u.s. team event . mayor Barbara Lee gifted her the key at the rally honoring the gold medalist .

content: manchester united are in pole position to secure champions league qualification . united have climbed to third place in the premier league after 19 points from 24 . despite the upturn in fortunes since carrick's arrival, uncertainty remains over his future .

title: ⚾ Passan's ultimate MLB season preview
content: the 2026 season is less than two weeks away, with the new york Yankees visiting the San Francisco Giants in a March 25 season opener followed by a full schedule of opening day games the next day . here is a team-by-team look at the year ahead for all 30 MLB teams .
